frameworks.name: sequential biases / trial-by-trial learning
frameworks.one_line: past trials and outcomes shape current choice via short-term updates

frameworks.name: win-stay/lose-shift heuristic
frameworks.one_line: spatial choice tendency based on previous outcome side

frameworks.name: value-based decision-making under risk
frameworks.one_line: choice between guaranteed and probabilistic rewards

core_question: Does lateral orbitofrontal cortex support trial-by-trial sequential biases in risky choice, and are different bias types neurally dissociable?
key_assumptions: trial outcomes are independent so any sequential effect is endogenous
neural causality can be tested with temporally precise optogenetic inhibition
risk and spatial biases are conceptually separable forms of history dependence
manipulations: explicit cueing of guaranteed vs probabilistic reward options
previous-trial outcome (risky win, risky loss, safe)
optogenetic inhibition of lOFC during choice reporting
key_findings: rats showed increased risk-taking after risky wins and spatial win-stay/lose-shift
lOFC neurons strongly encoded reward history and outcome receipt
lOFC inhibition during choice reporting abolished the risky-win bias but spared spatial biases
interpretations: different sequential biases have dissociable neural substrates
lOFC promotes learning of abstract, task-specific biases rather than spatial ones
